steps.title: Use quartic regression
steps.content: The expression has powers up to $t^4$, so Desmos can fit the result to a quartic. This avoids sign mistakes from subtracting the second polynomial.

steps.title: Enter values and fit the polynomial
steps.content:
Use five input values because a quartic can have five coefficients:
$$x_1=[-2,-1,0,1,2]$$
$$y_1=(6x_1^4-3x_1^2+3x_1-6)-(2x_1^4-4x_1^3+6x_1^2-6)$$
$$y_1\sim ax_1^4+bx_1^3+cx_1^2+dx_1+e$$

steps.title: Read the result
steps.content:
The regression gives $a=4$, $b=4$, $c=-9$, $d=3$, and $e=0$. So the equivalent expression is:
$$4t^4+4t^3-9t^2+3t$$
That matches choice B.

steps.title: Confirm the subtraction
steps.content: Distributing the minus gives $6t^4-3t^2+3t-6-2t^4+4t^3-6t^2+6$. Combining like terms gives $4t^4+4t^3-9t^2+3t$, so B is correct.
